claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists and requires web/fetch/search-capable tool support for external evidence during evidence gathering, with clear separation between project-scoped facts and external research.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch implementation exists with protocol definition for evidence gathering agents including explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles
evidence.why: Core implementation file defines the evidence dispatch schema and agent protocols

evidence.summary: Demand router implements careful routing for existing project facts and technical contract risks, requiring evidence-based PRD readiness
evidence.why: Router logic determines evidence policy and triggers cross_check requirements

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine defines evidence gathering requirements including scope separation (project vs external) and path-verified project facts
evidence.why: Documentation establishes the ground rules for evidence quality and scope

evidence.summary: Evidence directory structure exists at state root for storing agent results and artifacts
evidence.why: Artifact storage location confirms evidence system is in place

evidence.summary: External research URL provided in demand as example background evidence source
evidence.why: Demand explicitly requests using web/fetch tools on this URL as external evidence

risks: External web evidence could be mistaken for project implementation facts without proper scope=external labeling
Web/fetch tools might not be available in all runtime environments
Evidence agents might mix project-scoped path evidence with external URL evidence

result.notes: Core evidence dispatch files exist and demand scope is clear: external research must be marked scope=external while project facts require repo-relative path evidence. Ready for cross-checker verification.
